source.title: Dropped Cleanout Assembly - Diamond Ocean BlackHornet

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Mississippi Canyon Block 520

actions_taken: Safety stand down ordered at 11:20 hours
Safety meeting held with drill and crane crews
event.summary: On June 7, 2023, at 11:00 hours, a 43-foot cleanout assembly fell approximately 40 feet onto the pipe skate on the Diamond Ocean BlackHornet Drillship during drilling operations. The incident occurred because incorrect elevator inserts (4.5 inch EU instead of 4.5 inch IEU) were installed and the Driller and Assistant Driller failed to follow procedures and complete the Elevator Pre-use Checklist. A floor hand was in the red zone operating the pipe skate remotely at the time of the incident but was not injured. The incorrect inserts initially held hard-banded pipe successfully but failed when lifting non-hard-banded pipe.

threats.name: Failure to complete pre-use inspection checklist

consequences.name: Personnel injury or fatality - floor hand in red zone
Equipment damage - hoses and portable ladder

controls.name: Elevator Pre-Use Checklist (CHKL/001)
controls.barrier_role: Verification of correct elevator insert size before lifting operations
controls.lod_basis: Primary procedural control to verify equipment configuration before use

supporting_text: Elevator Pre-Use Checklist (ref# CHKL/001) was not filled out for this operation
This checklist had two items to verify the correct inserts are used: 1) Verify the size of the pipe that the elevators are to be used with. 2) Physically measure the internal bore of the elevators
Due to the Driller and Assistant Driller not following the guidelines and procedures and not filling out the checklist...the incorrect inserts were installed

controls.name: Assistant Driller Visual Inspection of Elevator Inserts
controls.barrier_role: Physical verification of correct insert installation and size
controls.lod_basis: Designated responsible person for carrying out equipment checks

supporting_text: The Assistant Driller conducted a visual inspection of the insert change and verified the installation was done correctly
The Assistant Driller took a glance at the size and observed 4-1/2 inch stamped on the inserts
Drill Pipe Elevators Guideline (5.1.4.8.a) Designate the Assistant Driller as the responsible person to carry out the checks
The Assistant Driller was not aware of his responsibility to ensure that the Elevator Pre-use Checklist was completed

controls.name: Driller Verification of Assistant Driller Checks
controls.barrier_role: Independent verification and oversight of equipment inspection
controls.lod_basis: Supervisory verification to confirm first-line checks completed

supporting_text: These checks are to be verified by the Driller
It is the Driller's responsibility to verify that the above checks have been carried out to his satisfaction
The Driller did not verify that the Elevator Pre-use Checklist was carried out to his satisfaction
The Driller and Assistant Driller did not follow the guidelines and procedures

controls.name: Drill Pipe Elevators Guideline (5.1.4.8.a)
controls.barrier_role: Procedural requirement for positive checks including actual measurements

supporting_text: Drill Pipe Elevators Guideline (5.1.4.8.a) Develop a specific procedure for inspecting elevators prior to use
Procedures will include positive checks, i.e., actual measurements, which override any temporary marks on equipment
The Driller and Assistant Driller did not follow the guidelines and procedures

controls.name: Change Inserts in Elevators Procedure (SSP180-DRL-13)
controls.barrier_role: Specific procedure for verifying correct insert size during change-out
controls.lod_basis: Task-specific procedure for critical equipment change

supporting_text: Change Inserts in Elevators Procedure (SSP180-DRL-13) Driller or Assistant Driller will verify the correct size inserts are installed
The floor hands were instructed to change the elevator inserts from 5-7/8 inch to 4-1/2 inch inserts
The incorrect inserts were installed which resulted in dropping the 43-foot cleanout assembly

controls.name: Toolbox Talk - Lifting Operations Planning
controls.barrier_role: Pre-job safety discussion and planning for lifting operations

supporting_text: At 07:00 hours, a toolbox talk was held with the Diamond drill crew, crane crew, and Schlumberger service hand for picking up the cleanout assembly
The drill crew discussed what would be picked up and how it would be racked in the derrick

controls.name: Tour Handover and Work Order Review
controls.barrier_role: Communication of upcoming operations and review of work procedures

supporting_text: The Diamond drill crew...came on tour at 02:00 hours after shortchanging (crew change mid hitch from days to nights)
As the crews exchanged, they discussed upcoming operations during the tour
Operational work orders that would be used that day were reviewed and signed by the drill crew

controls.name: Red Zone Access Control
controls.barrier_role: Prevent personnel from being in hazardous areas during lifting operations
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to limit exposure to dropped object consequences

supporting_text: There was one floor hand in the red zone at the time of the incident
This employee was operating the pipe skate with a remote which allowed this employee to walk into the red zone to get a better line of site
The floor hand stepped closer to the pipe skate to get a better line of site of the cleanout assembly being lifted
Given the weight and force of the 43 foot assembly falling from such a height, this incident could have caused a fatality

controls.name: Remote Operation of Pipe Skate
controls.barrier_role: Allow equipment operation from safe distance
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to enable distance from hazard zone

supporting_text: This employee was operating the pipe skate with a remote which allowed this employee to walk into the red zone
The floor hand quickly moved away and ran to a safe location

controls.name: Safety Stand Down and Incident Response
controls.barrier_role: Emergency response and learning from incident
controls.lod_basis: Post-incident response to prevent escalation and recurrence

supporting_text: At 11:20 hours, a safety stand down was ordered by the Diamond Captain
Both drill and crane crews had a safety meeting in the Driller's shack regarding on the incident and about moving forward

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
